findings.criterion: Behavior 5.1 — retrieve notes from localStorage and render one note per stored entry

evidence.quote: When the Notes App webpage loads, it must retrieve all saved notes from localStorage and display them accordingly.
Given the localStorage contains notes with values "Note 1" and "Note 2"
Then the page should display a note with text "Note 1" in a div with data-testid "note-main"

findings.reasoning: The Normal scenario provides a stored-notes precondition and asserts that each stored entry is displayed in a note main div (two concrete notes are checked). This establishes the retrieval-and-display behaviour at the UI level.

findings.criterion: Behavior 5.5 — no notes are shown when localStorage is empty

evidence.quote: If localStorage is empty, no notes should be shown.
Then no notes should be displayed on the page

findings.reasoning: The Edge scenario explicitly covers the empty-localStorage case and asserts that no notes are present, matching behaviour 5.5.

findings.criterion: Behavior 5.2 — per-note DOM structure (.note, .tools, edit/delete buttons, textarea presence)

evidence.quote: For each stored note, the app should dynamically create a div element with the class note, which includes a div with the class tools containing an "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button") and a "Delete" button (data-testid="delete-note-button").
Then the page should display a note with text "Note 1" in a div with data-testid "note-main"

findings.reasoning: The provided Normal scenario asserts that note text appears in a data-testid="note-main" div but does not assert the surrounding .note container, .tools subelement, the presence of edit/delete buttons (data-testid values), or the textarea element. These required structural anchors are therefore unproven by the scenario corpus and constitute a candidate gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Add/extend scenarios to assert per-note DOM structure: existence of .note wrapper, .tools container, buttons with data-testid "edit-note-button" and "delete-note-button", and a textarea with data-testid "note-textarea" for each stored entry.

findings.criterion: Behavior 5.3 & 5.4 — display mode for non-empty and empty notes (main visible / textarea hidden vs textarea shown)

evidence.quote: Notes retrieved from localStorage should be rendered with their content displayed in the main div, with the textarea hidden by default.
For notes that have no content, the application should instead show the textarea and hide the main div, allowing immediate editing.
Then the page should display a note with text "Note 1" in a div with data-testid "note-main"
Scenario: [Edge] Display no notes when localStorage is empty

findings.reasoning: The Normal scenario demonstrates that non-empty notes render their text in the note-main element, which partially covers 5.3. However, the scenario does not assert that the textarea is hidden for those notes. The provided scenarios do not include any case where a stored note has empty content, so behaviour 5.4 (empty-note shows textarea / hides main) is not exercised. Both behaviors are therefore only partially or not covered.
findings.suggested_fix: Add assertions in the Normal scenario to confirm the textarea is hidden for non-empty notes. Add a scenario where the stored notes array contains an empty-string entry and assert that that note shows the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") and hides the main view (data-testid="note-main").
